title: Convert Shipping Document PDF to Excel - Automate Supply Chain Data
metaTitle: Shipping Document PDF to Excel Converter | PDFExcel.ai
metaDescription: Convert shipping documents from PDF to Excel with AI. Extract bill of lading, packing slips, and manifest data automatically.
h1: Convert Shipping Document PDF to Excel
secondaryKeywords: bill of lading pdf to excel

hero.headline: Extract Shipping Document Data to Excel Automatically
hero.subheadline: Convert bills of lading, packing slips, and shipping manifests from PDF to structured Excel spreadsheets with AI-powered field extraction

summary: Converting shipping document PDF to Excel automates the extraction of critical supply chain data including tracking numbers, consignee details, cargo descriptions, weights, and delivery addresses. This eliminates manual data entry from bills of lading, packing slips, and shipping manifests while creating structured spreadsheets for inventory management and shipment tracking.
whoItsFor: Supply chain analysts tracking cargo movements
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing daily shipping manifests for inventory updates
Consolidating bill of lading data for customs reporting
Extracting packing slip details for warehouse management
supportedInputs: Scanned bills of lading and packing slips
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheet with tracking numbers, weights, and destinations
CSV file containing consignee details and cargo descriptions
commonChallenges: Manual typing of tracking numbers and addresses from shipping documents
Consolidating data from multiple freight forwarders with different document formats
Time-consuming extraction of weight and dimension data from packing slips
Error-prone manual entry of consignee information for customs documentation
howItWorksSteps: Upload your shipping document PDF, image, or scanned file
AI identifies and extracts key fields like tracking numbers, weights, and addresses
Review and customize which shipping data fields to include
Download the structured Excel file with one row per shipment or item
whyPdfExcelAiFits: AI recognizes standard shipping document formats from major carriers
Batch processing handles multiple documents from different freight forwarders
Custom field selection lets you extract specific data like hazmat codes or container numbers
OCR technology works on scanned shipping documents and mobile photos
limitations: Accuracy depends on document clarity - blurry shipping labels may need manual review
Handwritten tracking numbers or addresses have limited recognition compared to printed text
Complex multi-page manifests with nested cargo details may require field customization

faq.question: Can it extract tracking numbers from different shipping carriers?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI recognizes tracking number formats from major carriers like FedEx, UPS, DHL, and USPS, as well as ocean freight and rail tracking numbers from shipping documents.

faq.question: Does it work with scanned bills of lading?
faq.answer: Yes, OCR technology can extract data from scanned bills of lading and shipping documents. Clear, high-contrast scans provide the best accuracy for field extraction.

faq.question: Can I extract hazmat or special cargo information?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize field selection to extract hazmat codes, special handling instructions, and other cargo-specific data from shipping documents during the conversion process.

faq.question: How does it handle multi-page shipping manifests?
faq.answer: The system processes multi-page documents and can extract line items from detailed manifests, though very complex nested tables may need manual review for complete accuracy.

trustSignals: Files are encrypted during processing and deleted after conversion
exampleUseCases: Freight forwarder extracting consignee data from 50 bills of lading for customs reporting
Warehouse manager converting daily packing slips to Excel for inventory tracking
Logistics coordinator consolidating shipping manifests from multiple carriers into one spreadsheet
Supply chain analyst extracting container numbers and weights from ocean freight documentation
